functions.name: BE_RegularExpression
functions.description: A perl compatible regular expression testing function.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text to be examined.

parameters.name: expression
parameters.description: A "perl compatible" expression - see examples.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: A string of characters from the options below , eg "igm".

parameters.name: replaceString
parameters.description: If not empty, then a replace is performed rather than a find.

version_history.change: Added an extra option "v" to allow you to treat the input as a value list and perform the expression over all values in the list.

notes: Options :
* i - case insensitive
* m - multiline
* s - dot matches all characters, including newline<
* x - ignore whitespace
* g - replace all
* v - treat the input as a value list and iterate over each value.
More examples can be found here : [http://perldoc.perl.org/perlre.html#Regular-Expressions](http://perldoc.perl.org/perlre.html#Regular-Expressions)
functions.example_code:
BE_RegularExpression ( "abc 123 def" ; "[0-9]+" ) = 123
	BE_RegularExpression ( $list; "(?:\\S(?![ ]))+$"; "igm" )
To get a list of all the file paths that end in ".fmp12" : 
	BE_ValuesUnique ( BE_RegularExpression ( BE_FileListFolder ( $path ) ; "^.*\\.fmp12$" ; "v" ) )
The above example uses *BE_ValuesUnique* because the function leaves empty lines where the criteria doesn't match, so you'd have as many "values" as there were originally files, but anything not matching the criteria would be blank text in the list.

functions.name: BE_ScriptExecute
functions.description: Performs the script called *scriptName* in the current file ( if no fileName provided, or in *fileName* if available ).

parameters.name: scriptName
parameters.description: the name of the script to perform.

parameters.name: fileName
parameters.description: a filename of the file in which to locate the script.

parameters.name: parameter
parameters.description: the parameter to pass to the script called.

parameters.name: scriptControl
parameters.description: The values for this parameter are: Halt = 0, Exit = 1, Resume = 2, Pause = 3.

notes: Scripts called from the plugin are added to the script queue, so they run when the current script queue is empty.  In other words you can't have a calculation within a script, call a script using this plugin function, and have it start immediately.  It will run at the completion of the current script. Script results are not set and cannot be returned.
Be aware, there is an issue in the plugin API where filenames can only have a single "." in their name. Files with more than one will return an error 100 - File is missing.

functions.name: BE_ScriptStepInstall
functions.signature: BE_ScriptStepInstall ( name ; definitionXML ; id ; description ; calculation )
functions.description: Creates a new definition of a script step, using *definitionXML* with the name of the step as *name*.  We created this function because we weren't sure what of our functions should be exposed as script steps as well as functions.  This step lets us ( and you ) build new steps from existing functionality.  Then we can decide to make them permanent features ( or not ).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: the name of the new step.

parameters.name: definitionXML
parameters.description: the XML that defines the operation of the step.  Details about this are in the SDK, but we'll also try to locate some better documentation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: an internal id number for the step.  These can't be re-used.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: a text description that appears when you select the step in the script editor.

parameters.name: calculation
parameters.description: the calculation for taking the step details and turning them into a result.

notes: Calculation parameter :

You can reference the values returned by the UI parameters in your step, by using text like ###0### in your calculation.  Each parameter will be numbered from 0 to x and you just use ###0###, or ###1### or ###2### in your calculation and these will be replaced on completion.
XML Definition :

This XML is the text that will be passed to the scriptStepDefinition parameter of the RegisterScriptStep function. Up to ten script parameters can be specified in addition to the optional target parameter. All the parameters are defined with tags in a grouping.
The attributes for a tag include:
* Type - if not one of the following four types, the parameter is ignored
1. Calc - a standard Specify button that brings up the calculation dialog. When the script step is executed, the calculation will be evaluated and its results passed to the plug-in.
2. Bool - simple check box that returns the value of 0 or 1.

3. List - a static drop-down or pop-up list in which the id of the item selected is returned. The size limit of this list is limited by the capabilities of the UI widgets used to display it. A List type parameter expects to contain tags as specified below.

4. Target - will include a specify button that uses the new Insert From Target field targeting dialog that allows a developer to put the results of a script step into a field (whether or not it is on a layout), into a variable, or insert into the current active field on a layout. If no Target is defined then the result Data object is ignored. If there are multiple Target definitions, only the first one will be honored.

* ID - A value in the range of 0 to 9 which is used as an index into the DataVect& parms object for the plug-in to retrieve the value of the parameter. Indexes that are not in range or duplicated will cause the parameter to be ignored. A parameter of type Target ignores this attribute if specified
* Label - The name of parameter or control that is displayed in the UI

* DataType - only used by the Calc and Target parameter types. If not specified or not one of the six data types, the type Text will be used 1. Text 2. Number 3. Date 4. Time 5. Timestamp 6. Container
* ShowInline - value is either true or false. If defined and true, will cause the parameter to show up inlined with the script step in the Scripting Workspace
* Default - either the numeric index of the default list item or the true/false value for a bool item. Ignored for calc and target parameters

Parameters of type List are expected to contain tags whose values are used to construct the drop-down or pop-up list. The id of a value starts at zero but specific id can be given to a value by defining an "ID" attribute. If later values do not have an "ID" attributes the id will be set to the previous values id plus one.

functions.name: BE_ScriptStepPerform
functions.description: Performs a script step that was previously created with the *BE_ScriptStepInstall*, but performs it via id instead of via the usual Perform Script step.

parameters.name: scriptStepId
parameters.description: the id of the step to be performed.

functions.name: BE_ScriptStepRemove
functions.description: Removes the step definition from memory.  This will cause issues if you try to run a step with the previous definition.

parameters.name: scriptStepId
parameters.description: the id of the step to be removed.

notes: Be careful, there is no undo.

functions.name: BE_SetTextEncoding
functions.description: Sets the text encoding format for any function that writes or reads text on disk, for example the *BE_WriteTextToFile* function.

parameters.name: encoding
parameters.description: the encoding format to use. By leaving this parameter off, or setting it to empty you reset the encoding to its default.

notes: This is using the iconv library so the full list of encoding options can be found at
[http://www.gnu.org/software/libiconv/ ](http://www.gnu.org/software/libiconv/ )
or by typing "iconv -l" on the command line.

Note that if you've got content that does not match the encoding specified, the result may be an error or may be indeterminate depending on the library in use.  As a general rule we don't try to convert one set of text to another as the outcome may not be as the user intended.
If you're at all curious about encoding and why it's so complex, this is a good write up :
[https://tonsky.me/blog/unicode/](https://tonsky.me/blog/unicode/)
functions.example_code:
To use UTF_16 which is the default encoding of the XML DDR : 
	BE_SetTextEncoding ( "UTF-16" )
To use ISO-8859-1 which is a common windows encoding.
	BE_SetTextEncoding ( "ISO-8859-1" )
To revert back to default UTF-8 :
	BE_SetTextEncoding

functions.name: BE_Version
functions.description: Returns the plugin version number as text, eg 1.1.3
notes: This function should be not be used to when checking for latest updates, as the text string response of it is not easily compared. For example :
even though 10 is greater than 9.  To compare versions in order of release, use *GetAsNumber ( BE_VersionAutoUpdate )* instead and then use *BE_Version* to show a meaningful result to the end user.

functions.name: BE_VersionAutoUpdate
functions.description: The text of the plugin number, formatted as an 8 digit number, eg 04020400. This is broken into four parts, and so 4.2.4 would become 04020400 as 04 02 04 00. The final two digits come from a minor rebuild version, if there was one, when we did new builds but didn't change version number. This may be due to build issues or signing updates etc.
notes: This function should be used in place of *BE_Version* when checking for latest updates and comparing versions as numbers, as the text string response of *BE_Version* is not as easily compared. Use *BE_Version* when showing a meaningful description to the end user.

PDF.notes: All paths are native OS paths. BE_PDFAppend requires the destination file to already exist.

functions.name: BE_PDFAppend
functions.description: Joins two pdf files together, either by appending one to the first, or by creating a third PDF that is the end result.

parameters.name: pdfPathOrContainer
parameters.description: The first PDF file to be appended TO. Can be a container field or a path to a file.

parameters.name: appendPathOrContainer
parameters.description: The second file, to be added to the first file. Can be a container field or a path to a file.

parameters.name: destinationPath
parameters.description: the path to the result file.  If left out, then use this in a Set Field step that points to a container field.

notes: Some PDFs although they render properly in a PDF Viewer are incompatible with the PDF standard or at least with the version of the standard that our library supports. When this is the case you can get an error when trying to append the PDF.

To diagnose this before appending if you do *BE_PDFPageCount ( table::field )* on the PDF and you get 0 back from the plugin, then our library doesn't think there's any content, so it can't be appended.
You should be adding this check in to your script prior to using *BE_PDFAppend*, to confirm that both files contain data that can be read.
In terms of fixing the PDF, there's no way to do that inside the plugin at the moment.

You may be able to do this via an external library such Ghostscript. This option requires Ghostscript to be installed on your server or client, and then you run the command using *BE_ExecuteSystemCommand*.
There are other APIs that offer to do PDF repair such as :
[https://www.convertapi.com/pdf-to-repair#snippet=curl](https://www.convertapi.com/pdf-to-repair#snippet=curl)

Which can be called with the BE HTTP functions, or the native *Insert From URL* script step. However the extent to which they can repair a broken PDF vs a non standard PDF is not known. There may also be other similar services online, or local applications as alternatives.
functions.example_code:
Set a container field using two other fields.
	Set Field [ table::containerField ; BE_PDFAppend ( table::pdfField1 ; table::pdfField2 ) ]
Set a container field using two paths.
	Set Field [ table::containerField ; BE_PDFAppend ( "/pluginPath/to/file1.pdf" ; "/pluginPath/to/file2.pdf" ) ]
Create an external PDF file by combining a container field and a different external file.
	Set Variable [ $result ; BE_PDFAppend ( table::pdfField1 ; "/pluginPath/to/file2.pdf" "/pluginPath/to/outputFile.pdf" ) ]

functions.name: BE_PDFGetPages
functions.description: Copies a number of pages from the PDF at *pdfPathOrContainer* and creates a new document written to disk at *newPDFPath*, using only pages starting with page number *fromPageNum* and ending with page number *toPageNum* or the end of the document.

parameters.name: pdfPathOrContainer
parameters.description: The original file to GET the pages from, either a container field or a path.

parameters.name: newPDFPath
parameters.description: The path to write the result file to.

parameters.name: fromPageNum
parameters.description: The starting page number to begin from.

parameters.name: toPageNum
parameters.description: The page number to end on, or if left out will go to the end of the document.

version_history.change: Added a "garbage collection" option so that the resulting file size is reduced as well

notes: If the plugin can't read the file, but it is a PDF then you may get an invalid result from this function.  See the notes at *BE_PDFAppend* for more information.

functions.name: BE_PDFPageCount
functions.description: Counts the number of pages in a PDF file.

parameters.name: pdfPathOrContainer
parameters.description: The original file to count the pages of, either a container field or a path.

notes: If the plugin can't read the file, but it is a PDF then you may get a 0 from this function.  See the notes at *BE_PDFAppend* for more information.

Preferences.notes: Preferences persist in macOS user defaults / Windows registry between sessions. applicationID defaults to the plugin's own ID if omitted.

functions.name: BE_PreferenceDelete
functions.description:
The is the reverse of the BE_PreferenceSet function, this removes the preference at *key*, in *domain* from the system.

Preferences set via the BE plugin are available across all open copies of FileMaker applications.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: the key code for the value to set.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: the domain value of where to locate the preference file.

notes: If the domain is not included, there is a default used on Mac and on iOS :
au.com.goya.baseelements.plugin-user
On Windows the default will be :
Software\\Goya\\BaseElements\\PluginUser
( stored inside HKEY_CURRENT_USER ).
On Linux this function does not run, will return error 3 - Command is unavailable.

You can override the domain to specify a different file name on the mac, or a different path on Windows. To respect the various OS conventions you should use something similar to the above, but tailored to your solution.

This function is required in some situations as the user can't directly delete preferences files to remove a stored preference.  For example on the Mac, Setting a preference will create a corresponding file in the Users Library/Preferences folder.  However deleting that file does not delete this preference until logout, and it's possible that the preference file will be recreated from memory.

functions.name: BE_PreferenceGet
functions.description:
Gets the preference value stored by BE_SetPreference at *key*, in *domain*, from the system.

Preferences set via the BE plugin are available across all open copies of FileMaker applications.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: the key code for the value to get.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: the domain value of where to locate the preference file.

version_history.change: Return error 3, command not available on Linux

notes: If the domain is not included, there is a default used on Mac and on iOS :
au.com.goya.baseelements.plugin-user
On Windows the default will be :
Software\\Goya\\BaseElements\\PluginUser
( stored inside HKEY_CURRENT_USER ).
On Linux this function does not run, will return error 3 - Command is unavailable.

You can override the domain to specify a different file name on the mac, or a different path on Windows. To respect the various OS conventions you should use something similar to the above, but tailored to your solution.

functions.name: BE_SetPreference
functions.description:
Gets the preference value stored by BE_SetPreference at *key*, in *domain*, from the system.

Preferences set via the BE plugin are available across all open copies of FileMaker applications.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: the key code for the value to get.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: the value to store in this key.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: the domain value of where to locate the preference file.

version_history.change: Return error 3, command not available on Linux

notes: If the domain is not included, there is a default used on Mac and on iOS :
au.com.goya.baseelements.plugin-user
On Windows the default will be :
Software\\Goya\\BaseElements\\PluginUser
( stored inside HKEY_CURRENT_USER ).
On Linux this function does not run, will return error 3 - Command is unavailable.

You can override the domain to specify a different file name on the mac, or a different path on Windows. To respect the various OS conventions you should use something similar to the above, but tailored to your solution.

SMTP Email.notes: Setup sequence: BE_SMTPServer → BE_SMTPSetHeader (repeat) → BE_SMTPAddAttachment (optional, repeat) → BE_SMTPSend. Always check BE_GetLastError after BE_SMTPSend.

functions.name: BE_SMTPAddAttachment
functions.description: Adds the details of the file in the *attachment* container field to the list of attachments, for future *BE_SMTPSend* functions, to be sent with the *contentType*.

parameters.name: attachment
parameters.description: A container field to use as an attachment in an email.

parameters.name: contentType
parameters.description: A mime content type for the attachment.

version_history.change: Changed the *container* parameter to *attachment* and added the *contentType* parameter

notes: The *BE_SMTPSend* function lets you add attachments via a path whereas this function lets you select attachments from fields.  You can add multiple attachments by calling this function multiple times.  Once *BE_SMTPSend* is done it clears out the list of stored attachments regardless of the success of the Send operation.
Attachments are actually written to disk by the plugin, to the users temp folder, so this does require you to have access to a working temp folder ( defined by the OS ).
The *contentType* is what is set as the mime type for emails.  So usually something like :
Content-Type: application/pdf; charset=UTF-8
Content-Type: text/plain; name="file.txt"
Content-Type: application/octet-stream; name="file.pdf"
Possible mime types are found here :
[https://www.w3docs.com/learn-html/mime-types.html](https://www.w3docs.com/learn-html/mime-types.html)

The result of adding the attachment may be unknown until after the *BE_SMTPSend* function has run.  Use the *BE_CurlTrace* function and look for the section where the file path is referenced and any resulting error messages.

functions.name: BE_SMTPSend
functions.signature: BE_SMTPSend ( from ; to ; subject ; text ; { cc ; bcc ; replyTo ; html ; attachments} )
functions.description: Sends an email via SMTP. Use in conjunction with a call to BE_SMTPServer prior to calling this function. The To, CC, BCC and attachments parameteres can be passed value lists in order to send to multiple people or to include multiple attachments.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: the from name and email address.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: the to name and email address.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: subject line for the email ( a required parameter, but can be an empty string ).

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: text content of the email ( a required parameter, but can be an empty string ).

parameters.name: cc
parameters.description: the cc name and email address.

parameters.name: bcc
parameters.description: the bcc name and email address.

parameters.name: replyTo
parameters.description: the replyTo name and email address.

parameters.name: html
parameters.description: the text of the HTML version of the email content.

parameters.name: attachments
parameters.description: a list of file paths for attachments to be included.

version_history.change: Added an RFC 1123 format date header

notes: The content of emails on some servers appears to strip FileMaker line endings. Try replacing Char ( 13 ) with Char ( 10 ) in the email content.

Some servers, in particular various Exchange or Office365 based servers use a different authentication method. When attempting SMTP normally you may get a 35 error. You can force the correct method with :
BE_CurlSetOption ( "BE_CURLOPT_FORCE_STARTTLS" ; True )
before doing BE_SMTPSend.
functions.example_code: BE_SMTPSend ( "me@me.com" ; "you@you.com" ; "Subject goes here" ; $textContent  ; $cc ; $bcc ; "replyto@me.com" ; $htmlContent ; "/path/to/doc.pdf¶/path/to/secondDoc.pdf" )

functions.name: BE_SMTPServer
functions.signature: BE_SMTPServer ( server ; { port ; username ; password ; keepOpen } )
functions.description: Stores the SMTP connection details for future calls to the *BE_SMTPSend* function.

parameters.name: server
parameters.description: A domain name or IP address of the SMTP server to connect to.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: the port number ( a required parameter, but can be an empty string ).

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The userName if the url requires authentication.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: The password if the url requires authentication.

parameters.name: keepOpen
parameters.description: Whether to keep the connection open for future sends.

notes: ⚠️ Doc bug (parameter #3): parameters section names this parameter 'userName' but the signature and version history indicate the correct name is 'username'. Original as-fetched: 'userName'
This function doesn't connect to the server itself it only stores the connection details for future *BE_SMTPSend* function calls.
Authentication is controlled with curl, so additional options, other than the defaults can be set with the *BE_CurlSetOption* function, and headers can be set via the *BE_SMTPSetHeader* function.
Any future calls to this function overwrite any existing details.
port number can be left as an empty string, which will attempt to use whatever ports the server requires, default for SMTP is 25, but using SSL or TLS may use 465 or 587.
functions.doc_bug_note: ⚠️ Doc bug (parameter #3): parameters section names this parameter 'userName' but the signature and version history indicate the correct name is 'username'. Original as-fetched: 'userName'

functions.name: BE_SMTPSetHeader
functions.description: Sets a custom SMTP header for subsequent SMTP send actions.

parameters.name: header
parameters.description: header name.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: header value.

notes: A useful list of possible header values can be found here :
[https://www.iana.org/assignments/message-headers/message-headers.xhtml](https://www.iana.org/assignments/message-headers/message-headers.xhtml)
Some possible uses for this function are to apply headers such as :
X-Priority: 1 (Highest)
X-MSMail-Priority: High
Importance: High
The actual effect of setting this header depends on the both the mail server and clients.

Time.notes: All return integers. BE_TimeZoneOffset returns local UTC offset in minutes.

functions.name: BE_TimeCurrentMilliseconds
functions.description: Returns the current time as number of milliseconds. Effectively the same as GetAsNumber ( Get ( CurrentTimestamp ) ).

functions.name: BE_TimeUTCMilliseconds
functions.description: Returns UTC time as number of milliseconds - the same as the time from BE_TimeCurrentMilliseconds but in the UTC timezone.

functions.name: BE_TimeZoneOffset
functions.description: Returns the difference ( in minutes ) between UTC and local time.

Value Lists.notes: All operate on FileMaker value lists (newline-delimited). BE_ValuesSort type: 0 = text, 1 = numeric; order: 0 = ascending, 1 = descending.

functions.name: BE_ValuesContainsDuplicates
functions.description: Tests a list of values in FileMaker value list format and returns a True ( 1 ) or False ( 0 ) value if it contains duplicate values.

parameters.name: listOfValues
parameters.description: A FileMaker Value List ( ¶ separated values )..

parameters.name: caseSensitiveBoolean
parameters.description: Whether to treat "ABC" the same as "abc" or not.

functions.name: BE_ValuesFilterOut
functions.description: Does the opposite of the *FilterValues* function : it takes the *textToFilter* parameter, and removes anything in the *filterValues* list and returns the remaining values.

parameters.name: textToFilter
parameters.description: The list of values to start with.

parameters.name: filterValues
parameters.description: The values to remove from textToFilter.

parameters.name: caseSensitiveBoolean
parameters.description: Whether to treat "ABC" the same as "abc" or not.

functions.example_code: BE_Values_FilterOut ( "a¶b¶c¶d" ; "b¶d" ) = "a¶c"

functions.name: BE_ValuesSort
functions.description: Sorts *listOfValues* according to the ascending order *ascendingBoolean* and data *type*.

parameters.name: listOfValues
parameters.description: The list of values to start with.

parameters.name: ascendingBoolean
parameters.description: 1 for ascending, 0 for descending.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: A number corresponding to the value for different types.  At the moment, only two values are defined : 0 for Text, 1 for Numeric.

version_history.change: Added the ascending and type parameters

notes: In some situations this sort order won't be the same as the order that FileMaker would apply in a sorted field.  Different encoding and "type" arrangements treat values in different ways, so there's no expectation that the BE sort will be the same as the FM one - the technical details into why this is gets really complex as you get into text encoding and storage and values and ... If you're after a good explanation of why this is no longer a simple thing, this is a good reference : [https://tonsky.me/blog/unicode/](https://tonsky.me/blog/unicode/)

functions.name: BE_ValuesTimesDuplicated
functions.description: Select only those values from *listOfValues*, where the value has been repeated *numberOfTimes*.  In other words, filter the list to only those values that are repeated numberOfTimes.

parameters.name: listOfValues
parameters.description: The list of values to look through.

parameters.name: numberOfTimes
parameters.description: The count to check for in the list.

functions.example_code:
BE_ValuesTimesDuplicated ( "a¶c¶c¶d¶d" ; 2 ) = "c¶d"
	BE_ValuesTimesDuplicated ( "a¶c¶c¶d¶d" ; 3 ) = ""

functions.name: BE_ValuesTrim
functions.description: Trims leading whitespace from each and every value in listOfValues.  Works the same as if you called the regular Trim recursively on each GetValue ( listOfValues ; n ) and then re-assembled the list result.

parameters.name: listOfValues
parameters.description: The list of values to start with.

functions.name: BE_ValuesUnique
functions.description: Removes duplicate values from listOfValues.

parameters.name: listOfValues
parameters.description: The list of values to start with.

parameters.name: caseSensitiveBoolean
parameters.description: Whether to treat "ABC" the same as "abc" or not.

functions.example_code: BE_ValuesUnique ( "a¶a¶b¶b" ) = "a¶b"

Vectors.notes: Vectors are FileMaker value lists (one number per line). Useful for AI embedding similarity: dot product after L2 normalisation = cosine similarity.

functions.name: BE_VectorDotProduct
functions.description: Does a dot product of two vectors ( lists ) of numbers.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: the first list.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: the second list.

notes: For more information about vectors, have a look at :
[http://www.mathsisfun.com/algebra/vectors-dot-product.html](http://www.mathsisfun.com/algebra/vectors-dot-product.html)
functions.example_code: BE_VectorDotProduct ( "1¶3¶-5" ; "4¶-2¶-1" ) = 3

functions.name: BE_VectorEuclideanDistance
functions.description: The euclidean distance between two vector points.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: vector a as a list.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: vector b as a list.

notes: For more information about this see :
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_distance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclidean_distance)
functions.example_code: BE_VectorEuclideanDistance ( "1¶3¶-5" ; "4¶-2¶-1" ) = 7.07107

XML, XSLT, and JSON.notes: BE_XPath returns the first match; BE_XPathAll returns all matches as a value list. BE_XSLT_ApplyInMemory operates on strings; BE_XSLTApply reads/writes native file paths. BE_XMLValidate returns empty string if valid.

functions.name: BE_JSON_ArraySize
functions.description: Will return the number of values in a JSON array.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: the array to count.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: the path within the JSON to locate the array to count.

notes: ⚠️ Doc bug (parameter #2): parameters section names this parameter 'name' but the signature and version history indicate the correct name is 'path'. Original as-fetched: 'name'
If *path* is not included, it counts the array at the root of the JSON, and will return 1 for a non array type.
If *path* is included, the format is as per the *BE_JSONPath* function, which is NOT the same as the internal FileMaker JSON functions introduced in version 16.
functions.doc_bug_note: ⚠️ Doc bug (parameter #2): parameters section names this parameter 'name' but the signature and version history indicate the correct name is 'path'. Original as-fetched: 'name'

functions.name: BE_JSON_jq
functions.description: jq is a lightweight and flexible command-line JSON processor akin to sed, awk, grep, and friends for JSON data. It allows you to easily slice, filter, map, and transform structured data.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: the input to the jq function.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: the filter(s) to run on the input.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: one or more of the following characters: V c j r - see notes.

notes: More info: https://jqlang.github.io/jq/ and manual at https://jqlang.github.io/jq/manual/. Test filters at https://jqplay.org
c - compact: by default jq pretty-prints JSON output; this option puts each JSON object on a single line.
r - raw output (not quoted text strings): if the filter's result is a string it is written directly to standard output rather than formatted as a quoted JSON string.
j - join output: like r but jq won't print a newline after each output.
V - version info.
Not supported on Windows FMP.
functions.example_code:
BE_JSON_jq ( $json ; ".productTypes[].name" )
BE_JSON_jq ( $json ; "[ .prices[] | select(.quantity == 1 ) ] | max_by(.price) | .price" ; "r" )

functions.name: BE_XMLParse
functions.description:
Parses an XML file at *pathOrXMLText* to determine if it's properly formed. Does not check XSD details, just whether every tag is properly opened and closed.

The result will be empty when successful with a *BE_GetLastError* returning 0.

parameters.name: pathOrXMLText
parameters.description: the xml text or a plugin path to locate the file to parse.

notes: This function will look at the content of the text in the pathOrXMLText parameter, and if the first character is < then it assumes it is XML and treats it as such.  Any other first character means it assumes it's a path and will try to use it as a path on disk.

functions.name: BE_XMLStripInvalidCharacters
functions.description: This function removes characters considered invalid in XML from the file at *path*, and optionally writes the fixed file to the *resultFilePath*.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The path to the text XML file to read.

parameters.name: resultFilePath
parameters.description: If supplied, this will write the output to a new file. If not supplied then the original file will be overwritten.

notes: Used originally internal as a BaseElements developer tool function, likely not useful for anyone else.

functions.name: BE_XMLStripNodes
functions.description: This function removes the supplied list *nodeNames* of XML nodes from the source document at *inputFilePath* and writes the result to *outputFilePath*.

parameters.name: inputFilePath
parameters.description: the plugin path to the XML file to read from.

parameters.name: outputFilePath
parameters.description: the plugin path to write the output to.

parameters.name: nodeNames
parameters.description: a space separated list of xml node names to remove.

notes: Previously an internal hidden function, was made public in 4.2.0.
functions.example_code:
To remove the *HexData* and *PlatformData* nodes from an XML DDR file, you can use :
	BE_XMLStripNodes ( $inputFilePath ; $outputFilePath ; "HexData PlatformData" )

functions.name: BE_XMLTidy
functions.description: Does a "tidy" to reformat nicely the XML text.

parameters.name: xml
parameters.description: the xml text to be cleaned up.

functions.name: BE_XMLValidate
functions.description: Validates the XML in *xmlText* against an XSD *schemaText* for correctness.

parameters.name: xmlText
parameters.description: the XML text to be validated.

parameters.name: schemaText
parameters.description: the XSD schema text to validate against.

functions.name: BE_XML_Canonical
functions.description:
Generates the Canonical version of given *xml* document - Canonical XML is otherwise known as a "normal form" of XML, intended to allow relatively simple comparison of pairs of XML documents for equivalence.

Result is the output Canonical XML text.

parameters.name: xml
parameters.description: The text content of the XML to be used.

notes: Canonical XML is a specially structured version of XML that applies certain rules and will therefore be able to compared or used in certain validation processes.
See [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canonical_XML](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canonical_XML) for more information about Canonical XML.

functions.name: BE_XPath
functions.description: This finds the first instance of a node at the path *xpathText* within the text *xmlText*.

parameters.name: xmlText
parameters.description: the XML text to use.

parameters.name: xpathText
parameters.description: the path to the node you require.

parameters.name: namespaceListText
parameters.description: Namespace list as "prefix1=href1 prefix2=href2...".

parameters.name: asTextBoolean
parameters.description: Whether to return the XML nodeset as text instead of the standard XPath result.

notes: This function is based on the libxml library which only supports XPath 1.0 [http://xmlsoft.org]
functions.example_code: BE_XPath ( $XML ; "/abc:checkUserResponse/abc:result/abc:accountDetails/@type" ; "abc=http://xml.m4u.com.au/2009" )

functions.name: BE_XPathAll
functions.description: This finds all instances of a node at the path *xpathText* within the text *xmlText*.

parameters.name: xmlText
parameters.description: the XML text to use.

parameters.name: xpathText
parameters.description: the path to the node you require.

parameters.name: namespaceListText
parameters.description: Namespace list as "prefix1=href1 prefix2=href2...".

version_history.change: support objects of type XPATH_BOOLEAN, XPATH_NUMBER and XPATH_STRING
return an empty string when getting an empty node set as xml

notes: This function is based on the libxml library which only supports XPath 1.0 [http://xmlsoft.org]
functions.example_code: BE_XPath ( $XML ; "/abc:checkUserResponse/abc:result/abc:accountDetails/@type" ; "abc=http://xml.m4u.com.au/2009" )

functions.name: BE_XSLTApply

Zip and Gzip.notes: BE_Gzip / BE_UnGzip operate on container data (in-memory). BE_Zip / BE_Unzip operate on native file paths.

functions.name: BE_Gzip
functions.description: Compresses the file/text found in *data* and optionally applies the filename, otherwise uses the container data appended with the .gzip extension.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: a container field with an unzipped file in it, or text data.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: optional filename to use, appending the .gzip extension.

notes: This function should be used with a *Set Field* script step and the results stored as a container field.

functions.name: BE_UnGzip
functions.description: Uncompresses the file found in *gzip_data* and optionally applies the *filename*, otherwise uses the detail found in the container field if it exists.

parameters.name: gzip_data
parameters.description: a container field with an gzipped file in it.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: optional filename to use, appending the .gzip extension.

notes: If you're unzipping a file, then this function should be used with a *Set Field* script step and the results stored as a container field.  If you're expecting a text result then this could be used anywhere text is expected.

functions.name: BE_Unzip
functions.description: Unzips the archive file found at the path *archiveFilePath*. The unzipped file(s) are put into the same location as the zip file itself by default, or into the *outputFolderPath* if specified. Any existing file(s) will be overwritten.

parameters.name: archiveFilePath
parameters.description: a plugin file path, or a container field.

parameters.name: outputFolderPath
parameters.description: a system folder path to put the result..

version_history.change: added the option to allow archiveFilePath to be a container field

notes: ⚠️ Doc bug (parameter #2): parameters section names this parameter 'filename' but the signature and version history indicate the correct name is 'outputFolderPath'. Original as-fetched: 'filename'

If you're unzipping a file, then this function should be used with a *Set Field* script step and the results stored as a container field.  If you're expecting a text result then this could be used anywhere text is expected.
functions.example_code:
BE_Unzip ( BE_SelectFile ( "Select a Zip file to unzip." ) )
	BE_Unzip ( "/Users/nick/Desktop/MyFile.zip" )
	BE_Unzip ( "/Users/nick/Desktop/MyFile.zip" ; "/Users/nick/Desktop/ResultFolder")
functions.doc_bug_note: ⚠️ Doc bug (parameter #2): parameters section names this parameter 'filename' but the signature and version history indicate the correct name is 'outputFolderPath'. Original as-fetched: 'filename'

functions.name: BE_Zip
functions.description: Compresses the file(s) found at the *filePathList* and optionally saves the zip file to *archiveFilePath*.

parameters.name: filePathList
parameters.description: a plugin file path, or a container field.

parameters.name: archiveFilePath
parameters.description: a plugin file path to put the result.

version_history.change: modified the archiveFilePath parameter to allow a list of file paths, instead of a single file/folder
improved error handling, create empty directories in archives
don't crash when there's nothing to archive
return an error if more than one file with the same name is added to the archive
added the option to allow filePathList to be a container field

notes: The zip file format doesn't have a provision for adding multiple files of the same name. So if you run :
BE_Zip ( List ( "myfile.txt" ; "folder/myfile.txt" ) )
Then it will store both files in the zip, BUT when you unzip the second file will overwrite the first one. This is a limitation of the zip format, not a plugin limitation.
